title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: Complete the text with ONE word only.

content.text: Sleep is a biological necessity, yet its primary function remains (0) of the great unanswered questions in science. While we might feel that our brains simply shut (9) for the night, the reality is far more complex. Modern neuroimaging has shown that the brain is remarkably active during certain sleep stages. || (10) it not for this nocturnal activity, our ability to process memories and emotions would be severely impaired. Not (11) does sleep help consolidate what we have learned during the day, but it also flushes out toxins. (12) is widely believed that chronic sleep deprivation can lead to serious health issues. However, (13) the overwhelming evidence of its importance, many people continue to sacrifice sleep for work or entertainment. (14) such a habit to become permanent, the long-term cognitive effects could be irreversible. (15) far as current research is concerned, seven to nine hours remains the 'gold standard'. Ultimately, there is (16) doubt that prioritizing rest is essential for a productive life.

questions.explanation: Part of the phrasal verb 'shut down', meaning to stop operating.
Inversion in a second conditional structure (Were it not for...).
Part of the negative inversion 'Not only... but (also)'.
Dummy subject for the passive reporting structure 'It is widely believed'.
Preposition used to introduce contrast, followed by a noun phrase.
Inversion used to express a hypothetical condition (Were such a habit to become...).
Part of the fixed phrase 'As far as... is concerned'.
Part of the fixed expression 'there is no doubt that'.
